name: blocking-io-guard
tagline: Routes backend async blocking-IO findings to fix/guard/no-action and proves each fix with a red-then-green runtime anchor test.
verdict: Tight, deterministic SOP: concrete commands, explicit routing criteria, failure handling at every risky step, mandatory red/green re-verification, and a real references/ split keeping SKILL.md lean. No security issues. Weak spots: no explicit out-of-scope statement, uv/make invoked with no upfront availability check, and no version/changelog signal.

highlights.summary: No explicit 'NOT for'/out-of-scope language despite the SOP branching across Mode A/B and GUARD/FIX+ANCHOR/NO-ACTION routes.
`uv run --project backend ...`, `make detect-blocking-io`, and `make test-blocking-io` are invoked directly with no upfront requirements/availability check.
No version field or changelog anywhere in the file.
